page.title: Profile Engine Room
page.subtitle: Build, edit, and manage custom profiles. To switch profiles, use the Dashboard command center. Here you can CRUD profiles, set global launch automation, and backup/sync your configuration.
hero.title: Get ready to code in minutes
hero.subtitle: Create and configure custom development environments tailored to your stack. Add environment variables, manage credentials, and build the perfect workspace.
builder.empty: No custom environments yet. Click the button above to get started.
automation.title: Global Boot Automation
automation.subtitle: After the Setup Wizard is dismissed, optionally start your active compose stack or refresh the dashboard widget layout from disk. Requires Docker for compose; Flatpak needs socket access.
automation.composeUp.title: Compose up on launch
automation.composeUp.desc: Automatically start the active profile once per app launch.
automation.refreshDashboard.title: Refresh dashboard layout
automation.refreshDashboard.desc: Reload widgets from layout.json on launch.
backup.title: Backup & Sync
backup.subtitle: Export all profiles as JSON for backup and team sharing, or import profiles from a previous export.
btn.stackStubHint: Lightweight stack — click to enable full compose
btn.stackFullHint: Full compose stack — click to switch to lightweight
btn.setActive.title: Set as active profile in Dashboard
btn.switchStack.title: Switch to {{variant}} compose stack
wizard.general.title: General Information
wizard.general.subtitle: Choose a descriptive name and a base development stack for your environment.
wizard.general.templateLocked: Base template cannot be changed after creation to preserve workspace mappings.
wizard.general.descPlaceholder: e.g. My frontend workspace for project X
wizard.general.composeHint: Stub runs a minimal Alpine container. Full starts the complete stack (nginx, postgres, etc).
wizard.ssh.title: SSH Configuration
wizard.ssh.subtitle: SSH keys allow your development containers to securely authenticate and communicate with private repositories (e.g., GitHub, GitLab).
wizard.ssh.guideBeginner: • Beginners (Recommended): Use your default host SSH key (click the auto-detect button below or type host).
wizard.ssh.guideExpert: • Experts: Enter a custom SSH key filename located in your local ~/.ssh/ directory.
wizard.ssh.guideSkip: • Skip: Leave this field empty if you do not require repository authentication inside your containers.
wizard.ssh.keyDuplicateWarning: This SSH key is already used by profile "{{name}}". Each profile should have its own key.
wizard.ssh.generateFresh: Generate fresh key for this profile
wizard.ssh.scanning: Scanning your system for local SSH keys...
wizard.ssh.keyFound: Default SSH key found on your machine!
wizard.ssh.noKey: No default SSH key (id_ed25519) detected on your system.
wizard.env.title: Environment Variables
wizard.env.beginner.title: Recommended: Keep it simple!
wizard.env.beginner.desc: Environment variables define configuration values such as ports or application modes. **If you are unsure, you can safely skip this step!** The environment will run correctly using defaults.
wizard.env.expert.title: Expert Mode: Add variables manually or copy-paste raw .env file contents below.
wizard.cred.title: Credential Store
wizard.cred.beginner.title: Secure Secret Storage: Do you need it?
wizard.cred.beginner.desc: The credential store securely encrypts and stores access tokens or secrets (e.g. GitHub tokens). **You can safely skip this step if you are not working with private clouds or registries!**
wizard.cred.expert.title: Advanced Credentials Management: Define custom credential keys and secret values, or link previously saved secrets.
wizard.review.title: Review Configuration
wizard.review.subtitle: Check your configuration profile options before deploying.
msg.exportFallback: Clipboard unavailable; JSON put in import box below.
